topic: One Pi, five beds, one pump at a time — why not water everything at once
voiceover_script: Five garden beds, one pump, one bed watered at a time. Here's why that's the better design. A pump big enough to feed five beds at once is expensive, noisy, and mostly overkill for four fifths of the day. Instead, the Pi opens one bed's valve, runs a small pump, waters that bed properly, then moves to the next. A small pump delivers strong, even pressure to one bed easily — and the whole five-bed round still finishes in under fifteen minutes. Cheaper pump, quieter system, and every bed still gets watered properly, every single day. The full wiring and parts list for this exact setup is in the build plans — link in bio.
